summary: The fixation period for an invasive luxation typically ranges between six to eight weeks due to its association with joint instability and complex tissue damage requiring prolonged stabilization. This duration reflects standard clinical practice when managing traumatic disruptions that involve joints rather than isolated bone fragments.
